IRS990.MissionDesc: Defenders of Wildlife is a national, nonprofit membership organization dedicated to the protection of all native animals and plants in their natural communities.
IRS990.Desc: Conservation and Restoration of Imperiled Biodiversity in North America: Defenders of Wildlife focuses on the conservation of imperiled species and keystone or umbrella species that help restore the integrity and health of ecosystems. Our focus on wildlife and habitat conservation and the safeguarding of biodiversity defines our important niche in the environmental and conservation community. Long known for our leadership on endangered species issues, our programs emphasize imperiled species and their habitat throughout North America because of the irreparable loss that species extinction represents. We believe in the inherent value of wildlife and the natural world. To realize our conservation vision, Defenders of Wildlife engages in policy analysis

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: Mobilization of a Broader Constituency for Wildlife Conservation:Defenders of Wildlife implements nation-wide conservation initiatives and place-based conservation programs that are rooted in the communities in which we work. We focus on strategic areas where we can have a positive impact on wildlife and landscapes. We are driven by our responsibility to mobilize and grow a movement for conservation that will endure for generations to come. We issue a quarterly publication, DEFENDERS, sent to all members, which features informative articles on important wildlife and land management conservation issues, editorial comment, and updates on current activities taken by the organization. Our educational outreach projects include: providing the public with up-to-date information on wildlife and habitat protection

Form990PartVIISectionAGrp.TitleTxt: Sr VP, Conservation and Gen Counsel

OtherAssetsOrgGrp.Desc: Assets held in charitable remainder and other trusts

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The earnings of donor-restricted contributions can be used to fund either specific programs or general operations.
Management has evaluated the Defenders' tax positions and concluded that Defenders had no significant uncertain tax positions at September 30, 2023 and 2022.
Foreign expenditures are directly tracked and accounted for on the accrual method of accounting.
Conservation efforts in Mexico such as species, habitat conservation, and the progressive development of wildlife law and policy in Mexico.

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: To support tribal efforts to reduce conflicts between wolves and livestock and to assist with range and grassland conservation objectives.
To support the Arctic Refuge Defense Campaign's efforts to protect the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: The Blackfeet Tribe of the Blackfeet Nation

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: To install specialized bison fencing for their growing cultural herd.
To assist with costs associated with the Montana Beaver Conflict Resolution Program to promote coexistence with beaver in Western Montana.
To support landscape-scale conservation in southwest Florida.
To assist with costs for field technicians for the Southwest Montana DNA project.
To assist with costs associated with efforts to avoid livestock depredation by wolves in the Gila National Forest.
To assist with costs associated with a beaver conflict resolution position for MT Region 3, hosted by Montana Freshwater Partners.
To assist with costs associated with a beaver coexistence field technician in Northwest Wyoming.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Association for the Betterment of Bucksport

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: To conduct a hydrological evaluation of Bucksport.
To assist with costs associated with efforts to avoid livestock depredation by wolves in the Gila National Forest.
For Ranching with Wolves workshop in Oregon; towards continued wolf-livestock coexistence work in North Park, CO.
To support grizzly bear coexistence projects.
To support the production of a red wolf educational curriculum.
To support Combined Defense Project; to support GLT.
To support data analysis for the partnership research project; to support the analysis of data collected as part of a collaborative effort with Santa Fe County.
To assist with costs associated with a coordinator for the Cook Inlet Beluga Advocacy Group and its activities.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Fort Peck Assiniboine and Sioux Tribes

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: To assist with costs associated with installing bison fencing for the Fort Peck cultural buffalo herd.
For the annual Yellowstone bison transfer.
To support collaborative effort to stop environmentally damaging mining projects.
To support SSN.
To assist with riparian restoration efforts for beaver in American Prairie Montana.

To help implement projects for the benefit of eastern hellbender salamanders and other species; to assist with costs associated with hosting film screenings within the river basin; for the use and benefit of Tennessee River Basin Network.
For co-sponsorship of a New Member of Congress Reception; to support the ending of lease sales in the Arctic Refuge.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Friends of Laguna Atascosa National Wildlife Refuge

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: To support the 2023 Ocelot Conservation Festival; to support the "Earth Day Clean up at Laguna Atascosa NWR" event; to support the Latino Conservation Week events.
To assist with costs associated with efforts to avoid livestock depredation by wolves on the Ortega and Sponseller allotments and surrounding areas in the Apache-Sitgreaves National Forest.
To assist with costs associated with efforts to avoid livestock depredation by wolves in the Gila National Forest.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Slade Ranch LLC CO Sug Peters

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: To assist with costs associated with efforts to avoid livestock depredation by wolves on Greer and Voigt allotments and surrounding areas in the Apache-Sitgreaves National Forest.
To assist with costs associated with efforts to avoid livestock depredation by wolves on Strayhorse, Blue Ridge and South Escudilla allotments in the Apache-Sitgreaves National Forest.
To assist with costs associated with the workshop in New Mexico.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Nils Pedersen DBA Wind River Bear Institute LLC

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: Support for Salmonfest and Safety in Bear Country" program.
To assist with costs associated with Livestock Loss Prevention Program; to support costs associated with wildlife coordinator.

GrantsOtherAsstToIndivInUSGrp.GrantTypeTxt: To assist with costs associated with efforts to avoid livestock depredation by wolves.
To assist with costs associated with efforts to install bison fencing for the Blackfeet Nation's cultural buffalo herd.
To assist with costs to transfer Yellowstone bison to the Fort Peck Indian Reservation.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The Organization enters into a written grant agreement with the grantee that includes specific financial and programmatic accomplishment reporting requirements.

RltdOrgOfficerTrstKeyEmplGrp.TitleTxt: Sr VP, Conservation and Gen Counsel

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part III, Line 4a: Conservation and Restoration of Imperiled
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Biodiversity in North America: (continued) and advocacy, litigation, innovative science, technology development and conservation activities in the field. Our work is aimed at the prevention of species and habitats from becoming imperiled, the protection of endangered and threatened species and their habitats, and the restoration of the health of vulnerable species and their habitats. We are known for our innovative and pragmatic responses to a constantly changing political, economic and social environment. Our staff of scientists, wildlife policy experts, lobbyists, communications experts, and attorneys work in the halls of Congress, state legislatures, the courts, and local communities, and in numerous Defenders field offices across the country. We develop and advocate for innovative "up-stream" management solutions for public and private lands that encourage conservation of entire ecosystems and interconnected habitats. We emphasize collaboration with other groups as a means of bringing to bear as much collective influence as possible on policymakers and processes which are often subjected to relentless pressure from special interests. The shared goal of all of our activities is to make Defenders the nation's most effective champion of imperiled wildlife in an era of climate change and habitat loss, leading the way in meeting the challenges and seizing the opportunities that tomorrow presents to leave a rich and diverse natural legacy for future generations.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part III, Line 4b: Mobilization of a Broader Constituency for
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Wildlife Conservation: (continued) issues; maintaining an activist network and alerting activists to contact elected officials on key wildlife conservation issues; and providing information to students and teachers on wildlife issues. We also reach the public by briefing the local and national media on important wildlife topics, by producing high quality video and radio public service announcements on protecting America's wildlife and public lands and engaging supporters on our social network channels and though our daily blog. Defenders of Wildlife's website and social media platforms offer extensive information on wildlife species and related conservation issues. The website, www.defenders.org, serves as a resource for learning more about wildlife, understanding current policy issues, and getting involved.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 11b
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The Audit Committee meets with staff and auditors to review the draft 990 and address any Committee member comments. The staff then sends the draft 990 to all Board Members prior to finalization and submission of the 990 to the IRS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 12c
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The Organization annually requests completion of the conflict of interest form. The Board of Directors will take action, as deemed necessary, to address any potential conflicts.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 15a
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: For the President's salary, Human Resources independently collects salary survey information from other not-for-profit's as well as other Green groups and compiles findings. These findings are provided to the Organization's Executive Committee that then holds a closed door conference to establish the president's salary.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section C, line 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The Organization's governing documents, conflict of interest policy, and financial statements are available to the public upon request.
The Organization's Audit Committee assumes responsibility for oversight of the audit. This process is consistent with previous years.

IdRelatedTaxExemptOrgGrp.PrimaryActivitiesTxt: Protecting wildlife, natural habitats, and the environment.
